A.label: Fast lane
A.desc: Small, fully GPU-resident, sub-50 ms latency

B.label: Balanced
B.desc: Fits in 16 GB VRAM, the workhorse zone

C.label: Quality
C.desc: CPU offload, slow but highest accuracy

models.name: Qwen 3.5 4B
models.primary_use: ★ Incumbent fast lane — OpenClaw routing, fast tool calls, edge
models.note: Feb 2026 release, swapped into agentic_light on 2026-05-10. Hybrid Gated DeltaNet + sparse MoE on a 4B base, 262k native ctx, 201 languages incl. ES/CA. No new 4B-class challenger through July 2026.

models.name: Qwen3 4B (2507)
models.note: Solid small agentic model from late 2025. Dominated by Qwen 3.5 4B on context, multilingual reach, and quality.

models.name: Gemma 3 4B
models.primary_use: Fast multilingual triage, transcript shaping, Catalan
models.note: Best Catalan support of the small models. Weaker function calling than Qwen3.5. Useful as a CPU-side translation specialist on the 7800X3D.

models.name: Phi-4 Mini
models.note: Speed king in the small tier, but multilingual gap rules it out for ES/CA.

models.name: Llama 3.2 3B
models.note: Dominated. 2026 community testing reports unreliable triage / hallucinated replies.

models.name: Granite 4.1 8B
models.note: Late-Apr 2026 release; strong enterprise tool-calling in the 8B class (est. composite — headline numbers are coding-weighted, which this stack ignores). Dominated by Qwen 3.5 4B on speed and Qwen3.5 9B on quality at similar VRAM.

models.name: Ministral 3 14B
models.note: Dec 2025 release, checked this run for completeness (est. tps/quality). Good European multilingual incl. ES, 256k ctx — but dominated by GPT-OSS 20B and the tier-B MoEs on both chart axes.

models.name: Qwen3.5 9B
models.note: Confirmed 250k+ context on this exact GPU. Loses to Gemma 4 26B MoE and Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B on speed AND quality.

models.name: Gemma 4 12B Unified
models.note: Released 2026-06-03 — the one genuinely new frontier-relevant model this run. Encoder-free multimodal: text, image, and native AUDIO input straight into the backbone; 140 languages; benchmark coverage 'nearing the 26B' (est. composite 76, tps est. from Gemma 3 12B). Dominated on both chart axes by Gemma 4 26B MoE — its niche is the ~7 GB footprint and native-audio input, neither of which the current roles need. Watch for a future combined ASR+LLM lane.

models.name: Gemma 3 12B
models.note: Dominated by Gemma 4 26B MoE on every axis, and now by Gemma 4 12B Unified within its own size class.

models.name: Mistral Small 3.2
models.note: Strong European multilingual but dominated on speed by MoE alternatives.

models.name: GPT-OSS 20B
models.note: Excellent BFCL/τ-bench, native MXFP4 fits 16 GB. Multilingual ES/CA unverified — test before relying.

models.name: Gemma 4 26B MoE
models.primary_use: ★ Incumbent workhorse — transcripts, documents, deep agentic, EN↔ES↔CA
models.note: Still the standout 16 GB-class model as of July 2026. 99 t/s confirmed at full 256k context (njannasch.dev, 2026-03-02). Tie with Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B persists; nothing released May–July 2026 displaces either (GLM-5.2 and MiniMax M3 don't fit this box).

models.name: Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B
models.primary_use: ★ Tied with Gemma 4 26B MoE — agentic, long-doc, 201-language multilingual
models.note: Released 2026-04-16; still the newest locally-runnable Qwen as of July 2026 (no June small-model drop materialized). 98 t/s at full 262k context on the 5060 Ti. llama.cpp merged Qwen 3.6 MTP speculative decoding in May 2026 — a small single-stream tailwind, not a verdict-changer. Composite within ~1% of Gemma 4 26B → tie stands.

models.name: Qwen 3.6 27B (dense)
models.note: Released April 2026. Dominated by the 35B-A3B MoE sibling on speed AND quality at the same VRAM footprint — MoE wins on this hardware.

models.name: Gemma 3 27B (dense)
models.note: Dominated by Gemma 4 26B MoE: same family, higher quality, ~6× faster decode thanks to MoE.

models.name: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B
models.note: Predecessor to Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B. Dominated by the 3.6 update on speed and benchmark scores at lower VRAM.

models.name: Qwen3 32B (dense)
models.primary_use: Quality runs, hard agentic, batch overnight
models.note: BFCL v3 leader among open weights (75.7%). ~3.5 GB CPU spill, ~10–12 t/s. Held off the Tier B frontier by Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B which matches its quality at ~9× the speed.

models.name: Mistral Medium 3.5
models.note: Released 2026-04-29. Dense 128B is too large for this box even with 128 GB RAM offload — single-digit t/s. Modified MIT license has revenue gates. Dropped on hardware grounds, not quality.

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B
models.note: Heavy CPU offload (24 GB+ in RAM). Dominated by Qwen3 32B: faster AND higher quality at lower VRAM.

asr_models.name: faster-whisper Turbo
asr_models.tagline: ★ Drop-in upgrade from your current Turbo
asr_models.note: Same Whisper Large v3 Turbo weights you already use, reimplemented in CTranslate2. ~2× faster, lower VRAM via INT8. No quality regression. Use compute_type="int8_float16". Engine change still pending since 2026-05-10.

asr_models.name: Whisper Large v3 Turbo
asr_models.note: Solid for EN/ES transcription. Switch the runtime to faster-whisper for free speed; keep the model.

asr_models.name: faster-whisper Large v3
asr_models.tagline: ☆ Use for ES → EN single-pass translation
asr_models.note: Higher accuracy than Turbo and supports task=translate. Fallback for workload G when the LLM slot is busy.

asr_models.name: IBM Granite Speech 3.3 8B
asr_models.tagline: ☆ Accuracy upgrade if you hit Turbo ceiling
asr_models.note: 5.85% avg WER — top tier on the HF leaderboard. EN/FR/DE/ES/PT plus speech translation X↔EN. Heavier (~5 GB, ~3× the latency). Worth it only if Turbo is making errors that matter.

asr_models.name: Qwen3-ASR 1.7B
asr_models.tagline: ⏱ Watch — technical report out, ES comparison still thin
asr_models.note: Technical report published Feb 2026 (arXiv 2601.21337) with multilingual evals on Common Voice / FLEURS / MLS — but a head-to-head ES WER vs Whisper Turbo still isn't in the public leaderboard cut as of July 2026. Keep watching; smoke-test locally if transcribe quality ever becomes the bottleneck.

asr_models.name: NVIDIA Parakeet TDT v3
asr_models.note: v3 covers 25 languages including Spanish (HF Open ASR Leaderboard multilingual track). RTFx 2793 on the reference setup. Worth a smoke test on ES recordings — could be the speed king if ES quality holds. No new per-language ES numbers since the May run.

asr_models.name: NVIDIA Canary-Qwen 2.5B
asr_models.note: #1 on HF Open ASR Leaderboard at 5.63% WER, but English-strong with very limited Spanish support.

asr_translation_paths.label: Architecture (i) — Single-model
asr_translation_paths.headline: faster-whisper Large v3 with task=translate
asr_translation_paths.pros: Simplest pipeline, ~2 GB VRAM, ~30× realtime, single pass
asr_translation_paths.cons: Literal English output. No style polish, no summarization, occasional idiom loss.
asr_translation_paths.verdict: Fallback when LLM slot is occupied

asr_translation_paths.label: Architecture (ii) — Two-stage
asr_translation_paths.headline: faster-whisper Turbo (ES) → Gemma 4 26B MoE (translate + polish + disfluency)
asr_translation_paths.pros: Higher quality, context-aware translation, polishing + filler removal in same LLM call. Catalan extension comes free.
asr_translation_paths.cons: ~15 GB VRAM total — fast-lane model drops out for batches.

disfluency_verdict.decision: Fold into the LLM polishing pass
disfluency_verdict.reasoning: Specialized disfluency-removal models (DisfluencyFixer, DISCO) are research-grade and primarily EN/HI/DE/FR — Spanish coverage is sparse. Tier-B LLMs (Gemma 4 26B MoE, Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B) handle disfluency removal extremely well as part of an instruction-following pass. Adding a separate component means another model loaded, another VRAM slot, another point of failure, and worse multilingual coverage than the LLM you already run.
disfluency_verdict.prompt:
Clean up this transcript:
- Remove filler words (um, uh, eh, este, like, you know)
- Remove false starts and self-corrections, keep the corrected version
- Tighten conciseness without losing meaning or voice
- Preserve technical terms and proper nouns exactly
- Keep the output in [Spanish/English] — do not translate

Transcript:
{transcript}

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 1 — Two lanes
concurrency_recipes.note: Both at full speed. Total ~17 GB — minor shared-memory spill, handled gracefully on Blackwell.

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 2 — Qwen 3.6 stack (Apache 2.0 only)
concurrency_recipes.note: All-Apache stack — useful when license clarity matters (Macaya Bank work). Speed parity with Recipe 1.

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 3 — Quality batch
concurrency_recipes.summary: Qwen 3.5 4B (GPU) + Qwen3 32B dense (GPU + ~3.5 GB CPU spill).
concurrency_recipes.note: Fast lane unaffected. 32B at ~10 t/s — overnight transcript reprocessing.

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 4 — Three concurrent
concurrency_recipes.note: The 7800X3D's 96 MB L3 makes Gemma 3 4B on CPU run ~10 t/s — useful as a Catalan-strong specialist.

top_picks.why: Best small agentic model on this hardware as of July 2026 — and now the incumbent. Hybrid DeltaNet + sparse MoE, 262k native ctx, 201 languages, Apache 2.0. No new 4B-class challenger since February.

99 t/s, full 256k context, Google-grade multilingual including Catalan. Tie with Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B persists through July 2026 — keep Gemma as default on Catalan track record; switch to Qwen 3.6 if you need pure Apache 2.0.
BFCL leader at 75.7%. ~10 t/s with mild CPU spill — reserve for batch quality runs where correctness > latency.

agentic_light.reason: Incumbent (swapped in 2026-05-10) is still the Tier A Pareto pick: ~110 t/s, ~3 GB, 262k native ctx, 201 languages, Apache 2.0. Nothing new in the 4B class Feb–Jul 2026; Granite 4.1 8B and Ministral 3 checked and dominated on the no-coding composite.

agentic_heavy.reason: Incumbent and Tier B top pick; tie (≤3%) with Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B persists. The May–July releases don't move the frontier here: GLM-5.2 (744B) is a local NO-GO on hardware (#141), MiniMax M3 (428B) is borderline-loadable at 2-bit but coding-first and untested, Gemma 4 12B Unified is dominated by the 26B MoE on both axes. llama.cpp's Qwen 3.6 MTP support (May) is a mild tailwind for the Apache alternative if Catalan smoke tests ever show parity.

audio_transcribe.reason: Unchanged since 2026-05-10 and still pending: same Turbo weights via CTranslate2 give ~2× RTFx and lower VRAM at identical quality. Engine code change is manual (src/run_backend.py + src/backend_process.py). Qwen3-ASR and Parakeet v3 remain watch items — no new comparative ES WER published since May.

audio_translate.reason: Unchanged: the two-stage path (Turbo → Gemma 4 26B MoE) stays the default for ES→EN; whisper-medium remains a lazy-loaded fallback for when the LLM slot is busy. No new speech-translation entrant May–July 2026 changes this.
